description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with two large, symmetrical, crescent-shaped steel blades mounted on either side of a central shaft. The blades have a slightly marbled, metallic finish and appear sharp along their outer edges, with a robust central mounting. The handle is cylindrical, made of wood with a visible grain, and is reinforced with metallic bands and decorative golden end caps. The grip section is textured for better handling, and the overall design is both functional and ornamental, suggesting it could be used for ceremonial or display purposes as well as practical tasks. The axe is proportioned for two-handed use, with a balanced weight distribution between the handle and the blades. No visible branding or markings are present, and the construction appears sturdy and traditional.
description: A double-bladed steel axe with a wooden handle, metallic bands, and gold-toned end caps, designed for two-handed use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, symmetrical steel blades and the full length of the wooden handle with gold-toned end caps.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with metallic bands and end caps visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the mirrored double-blade design and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the handle's cylindrical shape.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with visible grain, metallic bands, and gold-toned rounded end caps; textured grip section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical crescent-shaped steel blades with sharp outer edges and a robust central mounting.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional mounting with the blade wedged and reinforced by metallic bands.
